title: Area of composite shapes
description: Practice finding the areas of complex shapes that are composed of smaller shapes.

assessment_items.question_content:
There is a pair of parallel sides in the following shape.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the area of the shape?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{units}^2$
hints:
###Strategy

Area tells us the amount of space enclosed by the figure. 

There are many ways we could split up the space into simpler shapes. Let's split the figure into a rectangle and a triangle.

[[☃ image 1]]

We can find the area of each shape, then add them to find the total area.

###Area of the rectangle

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\text{Area}_\text{rectangle} &= \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
&= 2 \cdot 12\\\\
&= 24
\end{align}$

The rectangle has an area of $\blueE{24}$ square units.

###Area of the triangle

The base of the triangle is $5$ units, because $7-2=5$.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\text{Area}_\text{triangle} &= \dfrac{1}{2} \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
&= \dfrac{1}{2} \cdot 5 \cdot 12\\\\
&= 30
\end{align}$

The triangle has an area of $\maroonD{30}$ square units.

###Total area

To find the total area of the shape, we can add the two partial areas.

$\blueE{24}+\maroonD{30}=\purpleD{54}$
The area of the shape is $54 \text{ units}^2$.

options.alt: A quadrilateral with 1 pair of parallel sides. The top base is 2 units. The bottom base is 7 units. The height is 12 units. The length of the longest side is 13 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
There is a pair of parallel sides in the following shape.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the area of the shape?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{units}^2$
hints:
###Strategy

Area tells us the amount of space enclosed by the figure. 

There are many ways we could split up the space into simpler shapes. Let's split the figure into a rectangle and a triangle.

[[☃ image 1]]

We can find the area of each shape, then add them to find the total area.

###Area of the rectangle

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\text{Area}_\text{rectangle} &= \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
&= 3 \cdot 3\\\\
&= 9
\end{align}$

The rectangle has an area of $\blueE{9}$ square units.

###Area of the triangle

The base of the triangle is $4$ units, because $7-3=4$.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\text{Area}_\text{triangle} &= \dfrac{1}{2} \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
&= \dfrac{1}{2} \cdot 4 \cdot 3\\\\
&= 6
\end{align}$

The triangle has an area of $\redE{6}$ square units.

###Total area

To find the total area of the shape, we can add the two partial areas.

$\blueE{9}+\redE{6}=\purpleD{15}$
The area of the shape is $15 \text{ units}^2$.

options.alt: A quadrilateral with 1 pair of parallel sides. The top base is 7 units. The bottom base is 3 units. The height is 3 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
There is a pair of parallel sides in the following shape.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the area of the shape?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{units}^2$
hints:
###Strategy

Area tells us the amount of space enclosed by the figure. 

There are many ways we could split up the space into simpler shapes. Let's split the figure into a rectangle with a triangle removed from it.

[[☃ image 1]]

We can find the area of each shape, then subtract them to find the total area.

###Area of the rectangle

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\text{Area}_\text{rectangle} &= \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
&= 10 \cdot 24\\\\
&= 240
\end{align}$

The rectangle has an area of $\blueE{240}$ square units.

###Area of the triangle

The base of the triangle is $7$ units, because $10-3=7$.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\text{Area}_\text{triangle} &= \dfrac{1}{2} \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
&= \dfrac{1}{2} \cdot 7 \cdot 24\\\\
&= 84
\end{align}$

The triangle has an area of $\maroonD{84}$ square units.

###Total area

To find the total area of the shape, we can subtract the  removed area from the larger area.

$\blueE{240}-\maroonD{84}=\purpleD{156}$
The area of the shape is $156 \text{ units}^2$.

options.alt: A quadrilateral with 1 pair of parallel sides. The top base is 3 units. The bottom base is 10 units. The height is 24 units. The length of the longest side is 25 units.

assessment_items.question_content:

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{ units}^2$
hints:
###Strategy

Area tells us the amount of space enclosed by the figure. 

There are many ways we could split up the space into simpler shapes. Let's split the figure into two triangles.

[[☃ image 1]]

We can find the area of each triangle, then add them to find the total area.

###Area of the triangle on the left

[[☃ image 1]]

The triangles have the same height.

$\begin{align}
\text{Area}_\text{triangle} &= \dfrac{1}{2} \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
&= \dfrac{1}{2} \cdot 2 \cdot 2\\\\
&= 2
\end{align}$

The triangle on the left has an area of $\blueE{2}$ square units.

###Area of the triangle on the right

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\text{Area}_\text{triangle} &= \dfrac{1}{2} \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
&= \dfrac{1}{2} \cdot 10 \cdot 2\\\\
&= 10
\end{align}$

The triangle on the right has an area of $\maroonD{10}$ square units.

###Total area

To find the total area of the shape, we can add the two partial areas.

$\blueE{2}+\maroonD{10}=\purpleD{12}$
The area of the shape is $12 \text{ units}^2$.

options.alt: A quadrilateral with 1 pair of parallel sides. The bottom base is 2 units. The top base is 10 units. The height is 2 units. There is a dashed line that is perpendicular to the base. There is an arrow on the top that begins at the top, left vertex and ends at the top, right vertex.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following shape has $2$ pairs of parallel sides.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the area of the shape?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{units}^2$
hints:
###Strategy

Area tells us the amount of space enclosed by the figure. 

There are many ways we could split up the space into simpler shapes. Let's split the figure into a rectangle with a triangle removed from it.

[[☃ image 1]]

We can find the area of each shape, then subtract them to find the total area.

###Area of the rectangle

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\text{Area}_\text{rectangle} &= \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
&= 4 \cdot 8\\\\
&= 32
\end{align}$

The rectangle has an area of $\blueE{32}$ square units.

###Area of the triangle

The base of the triangle is $2$ units, because $4-2=2$. The height of the triangle is $4$ units, because $8-4=4$.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\text{Area}_\text{triangle} &= \dfrac{1}{2} \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
&= \dfrac{1}{2} \cdot 2 \cdot 4\\\\
&= 4
\end{align}$

The triangle has an area of $\maroonD{4}$ square units.

###Total area

To find the total area of the shape, we can subtract the  removed area from the larger area.

$\blueE{32}-\maroonD{4}=\purpleD{28}$
The area of the shape is $28 \text{ units}^2$.

options.alt: A 5 sided figure with 2 pairs of parallel sides. The top base is 4 units. The bottom base is 2 units. The right side is 4 units. The left side is 8 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following shape has $1$ pair of parallel sides.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the area of the shape?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{units}^2$
hints:
###Strategy

Area tells us the amount of space enclosed by the figure. 

There are many ways we could split up the space into simpler shapes. Let's split the figure into a rectangle with a triangle removed from it.

[[☃ image 1]]

We can find the area of each shape, then subtract them to find the total area.

###Area of the rectangle

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\text{Area}_\text{rectangle} &= \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
&= 7 \cdot 10\\\\
&= 70
\end{align}$

The rectangle has an area of $\blueE{70}$ square units.

###Area of the triangle

The height of the triangle is $4$ units, because $10-6=4$.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\text{Area}_\text{triangle} &= \dfrac{1}{2} \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
&= \dfrac{1}{2} \cdot 7 \cdot 4\\\\
&= 14

The triangle has an area of $\maroonD{14}$ square units.

###Total area

To find the total area of the shape, we can subtract the  removed area from the larger area.

$\blueE{70}-\maroonD{14}=\purpleD{56}$
The area of the shape is $56 \text{ units}^2$.

options.alt: A 5 sided figure made of a rectangle with a triangle removed from the top. The base of the rectangle is 7 units and the height is 10 units. A dashed line parallel to the height of the rectangle runs from the vertex of the missing triangle to the base. The dashed line is 6 units long.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following shape has $2$ pairs of parallel sides.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the area of the shape?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{units}^2$
hints:
###Strategy

Area tells us the amount of space enclosed by the figure. 

There are many ways we could split up the space into simpler shapes. Let's split the figure into a rectangle with $2$ triangles removed from it.

[[☃ image 1]]

We can find the area of each shape, then subtract the smaller areas to find the total area.

###Area of the rectangle

[[☃ image 1]]

The height of the rectangle is $9$ units long because $5+4=9$.

$\begin{align}
\text{Area}_\text{rectangle} &= \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
&= 9 \cdot 9\\\\
&= 81
\end{align}$

The rectangle has an area of $\blueE{81}$ square units.

###Area of the triangle on the left

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\text{Area}_\text{triangle} &= \dfrac{1}{2} \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
&= \dfrac{1}{2} \cdot 3 \cdot 4\\\\
&= 6
\end{align}$

The triangle on the left has an area of $\maroonD{6}$ square units.

###Area of the triangle on the right

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\text{Area}_\text{triangle} &= \dfrac{1}{2} \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
&= \dfrac{1}{2} \cdot 4 \cdot 5\\\\
&= 10
\end{align}$

The triangle on the right has an area of $\goldE{10}$ square units.

###Total area

To find the total area of the shape, we can subtract the  removed areas from the larger area.

$\blueE{81}-\maroonD{6}-\goldE{10}=\purpleD{65}$
The area of the shape is $65 \text{ units}^2$.

options.alt: A 5 sided figure made of a rectangle with a triangle removed from the bottom, left side and bottom, right side. The base of the rectangle is 9 units. The left side length of the figure is 5 units. The right side length of the figure is 4 units.  A dashed line parallel to the height of the rectangle and a dashed line perpendicular to the height create a right triangle in the bottom, left and bottom, right side. The base of the left most right triangle is 3 units and the height is 4 units. The base of the right most right triangle is 4 units and the height is 5 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following shape has $1$ pair of parallel sides.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the area of the shape?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{units}^2$
hints:
###Strategy

Area tells us the amount of space enclosed by the figure. 

There are many ways we could split up the space into simpler shapes. Let's split the figure into a rectangle and $2$ triangles.

[[☃ image 1]]

We can find the area of each shape, then add them to find the total area.

###Area of the top triangle

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\text{Area}_\text{triangle} &= \dfrac{1}{2} \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
&= \dfrac{1}{2} \cdot 8 \cdot 2\\\\
&= 8
\end{align}$

The top triangle has an area of $\blueE{8}$ square units.

###Area of the rectangle

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\text{Area}_\text{rectangle} &= \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
&= 8 \cdot 7\\\\
&= 56
\end{align}$

The rectangle has an area of $\maroonD{56}$ square units.

###Area of the bottom triangle

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\text{Area}_\text{triangle} &= \dfrac{1}{2} \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
&= \dfrac{1}{2} \cdot 8 \cdot 3\\\\
&= 12
\end{align}$

The bottom triangle has an area of $\goldE{12}$ square units.

###Total area

To find the total area of the shape, we can add the three partial areas.

$\blueE{8}+\maroonD{56}+\goldE{12}=\purpleD{76}$
The area of the shape is $76 \text{ units}^2$.

options.alt: A 6 sided figure made of a rectangle with a right triangle removed from the top, left side, top, right side, bottom, left side, and bottom, right side.  This creates a figure of a rectangle with a triangle attached to the top and the bottom. The height of the triangle attached to the top of the rectangle is 2 units. The height of the rectangle is 7 units. The height of the triangle attached to the bottom of the rectangle is 3 units. The base of the rectangle is 8 units. There is an arrow on the left that begins at the bottom of the figure and ends where the bottom triangle meets the base of the rectangle. There is an arrow on the left that begins at the base of the rectangle and ends at the top of the rectangle. There is an arrow that begins as the top of the rectangle and ends at the top of the figure.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following shape has $1$ pair of parallel sides.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the area of the shape?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{units}^2$
hints:
###Strategy

Area tells us the amount of space enclosed by the figure. 

There are many ways we could split up the space into simpler shapes. Let's split the figure into a rectangle and a triangle.

[[☃ image 1]]

We can find the area of each shape, then add them to find the total area.

###Area of the triangle

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\text{Area}_\text{triangle} &= \dfrac{1}{2} \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
&= \dfrac{1}{2} \cdot 9 \cdot 3\\\\
&= 13.5
\end{align}$

The triangle has an area of $\blueE{13.5}$ square units.

###Area of the rectangle

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\text{Area}_\text{rectangle} &= \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
&= 9 \cdot 6\\\\
&= 54
\end{align}$

The rectangle has an area of $\maroonD{54}$ square units.

###Total area

To find the total area of the shape, we can add the two partial areas.

$\blueE{13.5}+\maroonD{54}=\purpleD{67.5}$
The area of the shape is $67.5 \text{ units}^2$.

options.alt: A 5 sided figure made of a rectangle with a right triangle removed from the top, left side and the top, right side.  This creates a figure of a rectangle with 2 right triangles attached to the top. The height of the right triangles attached to the top of the rectangle are 3 units. The base of the rectangle is 9 units. The height of the rectangle is 6 units. There is a dashed line that is parallel to the base along the top of the rectangle and the base of the right triangles. assessment_items.question_content:
The following shape has $0$ pairs of parallel sides.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the area of the shape?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{units}^2$
hints:
###Strategy

Area tells us the amount of space enclosed by the figure. 

There are many ways we could split up the space into simpler shapes. Let's split the figure into three triangles.

[[☃ image 1]]

We can find the area of each shape, then add them to find the total area.

###Area of the top triangle

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\text{Area}_\text{triangle} &= \dfrac{1}{2} \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
&= \dfrac{1}{2} \cdot 10 \cdot 7\\\\
&= 35
\end{align}$

The top triangle has an area of $\blueE{35}$ square units.

###Area of the middle triangle

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\text{Area}_\text{triangle} &= \dfrac{1}{2} \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
&= \dfrac{1}{2} \cdot 10 \cdot 5\\\\
&= 25
\end{align}$

The middle triangle has an area of $\maroonD{25}$ square units.

###Area of the bottom triangle

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\text{Area}_\text{triangle} &= \dfrac{1}{2} \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
&= \dfrac{1}{2} \cdot 3 \cdot 5\\\\
&= 7.5
\end{align}$

The bottom triangle has an area of $\goldE{7.5}$ square units.

###Total area

To find the total area of the shape, we can add the three partial areas.

$\blueE{35}+\maroonD{25}+\goldE{7.5}=\purpleD{67.5}$
The area of the shape is $67.5 \text{ units}^2$.

The height of the top right triangle is 7 units and the base is 10 units. The height of the bottom right triangle is 5 units. The bottom base of the quadrilateral with 1 pair of parallel sides is 3 units.

assessment_items.question_content:
The following shape has $1$ pair of parallel sides.

[[☃ image 1]]

**What is the area of the shape?**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] $\text{units}^2$
hints:
###Strategy

Area tells us the amount of space enclosed by the figure. 

There are many ways we could split up the space into simpler shapes. Let's split the figure into $2$ triangles and a rectangle.

[[☃ image 1]]

We can find the area of each shape, then add them to find the total area.

###Area of the triangle on the left

[[☃ image 1]]

The height of the triangle is $8$ units long because $5+3=8$.

$\begin{align}
\text{Area}_\text{triangle} &= \dfrac{1}{2} \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
&= \dfrac{1}{2} \cdot 8 \cdot 8\\\\
&= 32
\end{align}$

The triangle on the left has an area of $\blueE{32}$ square units.

###Area of the rectangle

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\text{Area}_\text{rectangle} &= \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
&= 2 \cdot 5\\\\
&= 10
\end{align}$

The rectangle has an area of $\maroonD{10}$ square units.

###Area of the triangle on the right

[[☃ image 1]]

$\begin{align}
\text{Area}_\text{triangle} &= \dfrac{1}{2} \cdot \text{base} \cdot \text{height}\\\\
&= \dfrac{1}{2} \cdot 6 \cdot 5\\\\
&= 15
\end{align}$

The triangle on the right has an area of $\goldE{15}$ square units.

###Total area

To find the total area of the shape, we can add the three partial areas.

$\blueE{32}+\maroonD{10}+\goldE{15}=\purpleD{57}$
The area of the shape is $57 \text{ units}^2$.

options.alt: A 5 sided figure split into 3 sections by dashed lines. The base of the figure is 16 units and the height is 8 units. The base of the first right triangle is 8 units and the height is 8 units. The base of the rectangle is 2 units and the height is 5 units. The base of the second right triangle is 6 units and the height is 5 units.
